authorityRule: Documents 01-14 govern requirements; Document 15 reports implementation status.

requirements.title: Account and Workspace Readiness
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate that an eligible Visitor can create an account, complete required verification, authenticate, receive exactly one personal workspace, and retrieve that workspace without duplicate provisioning.

requirements.title: Workspace Isolation
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate that users, queries, caches, jobs, files, indexes, exports, provider connections, and runtime executions cannot access another workspace’s protected data without explicit authorization.

requirements.title: Provider Connection
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate protected provider-credential submission, validation, masked display, replacement, disabling, disconnection, and deletion without plaintext credential exposure.

requirements.title: Conversational Execution
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate conversation creation, idempotent message acceptance, runtime execution, provider dispatch, streamed response delivery, durable assistant-message finalization, cancellation, retry, and stable failure reporting.

requirements.title: Context and Prompt Control
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate deterministic instruction precedence, context-budget enforcement, context deduplication, user-intent preservation, provider-limit compliance, and traceability of selected context.

requirements.title: Structured Memory
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate candidate extraction, confirmation, rejection, activation, deactivation, retrieval, inspection, update, conflict handling, soft deletion, restoration, permanent deletion, and provenance reconciliation.

requirements.title: Rapid-Fire Message Responsiveness
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate that Message B is not blocked by unfinished memory or knowledge processing for Message A, that Message B uses the committed context snapshot available when its execution begins, and that later committed memory becomes eligible only for subsequent executions.

requirements.title: Usage and Cost Visibility
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate authorized PDF and TXT upload, trusted type detection, bounded parsing, segmentation, indexing, search, runtime injection, provenance recording, and complete deletion of derived representations.

requirements.title: Per-Request Token Display
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate source-authorized knowledge extraction, deduplication, retrieval, provenance, reprocessing, source-deletion reconciliation, and idempotent processing.

requirements.title: Per-Request Cost Display
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate input, output, and total-token recording; cost estimation; pricing-version association; failed and cancelled usage recording; aggregation; and authorized inspection.

requirements.title: Export and Deletion
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate soft-warning behaviour, hard-ceiling blocking, concurrent budget reservation, quota validation, rate limiting, and stable user-safe errors.

requirements.title: Export Request
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate authorized export generation, scope integrity, secret exclusion, time-limited download, expiration, account and workspace deletion, derived-data deletion, backup-restoration deletion replay, idempotency, and failure reconciliation.

requirements.title: Deletion Request
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate elevated administrator authentication, least-privilege authorization, restricted support access, immutable audit records, provider-state administration, and privacy-preserving administrative operations.

requirements.title: Audit Trail
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate authorized, preference-aware, privacy-minimized, idempotent notification delivery for security, usage, export, restriction, and deletion events.

requirements.title: Error Safety
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate explicit job states, workspace-scope validation, deduplication, bounded retries, stale-job recovery, cancellation, late-result rejection, and failure isolation.

requirements.title: Audit and Error Safety
requirements.statement: The MVP shall demonstrate stable error categories, privacy-preserving user messages, correlation identifiers, secret exclusion, and auditable security-significant and destructive actions.

requirements.title: Snapshot Lock for Rapid-Fire Messages
requirements.statement: If Message B arrives while Message A background processing is incomplete, Message B must be accepted and processed using the pre-existing context snapshot without waiting. Newly extracted memories become active only after their background transaction completes, becoming eligible for Message C onward.
acceptanceCriteria: Context snapshot captured at exact moment
Message A background results inactive in Message B
Message A results active in Message C
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Ten Trust Boundary Controls
requirements.statement: Every trust boundary crossing shall enforce: authenticated principal identity, authorization/scope, input validation, content-type/file-type validation, rate/quota/abuse controls, encryption in transit, replay protection, idempotency, correlation/audit IDs, data minimization, normalized error handling, timeout/retry/circuit-breaking, egress allowlisting, credential isolation, security monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Each boundary control verifiable via test or inspection
securityConstraints: Controls shall fail closed when integrity cannot be established
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Content Trust Hierarchy — Instruction Precedence
requirements.statement: When content sources conflict, the following precedence applies: 1. Mandatory platform policy; 2. Safety instructions; 3. Organization policy; 4. Workspace instructions; 5. Project instructions; 6. Conversation instructions; 7. Current user instructions; 8. Retrieved contextual data; 9. Provider-generated data. Retrieved content shall not automatically become trusted instructions.
securityConstraints: Platform policy and safety instructions always take precedence
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Runtime Execution State Machine
requirements.statement: Every runtime execution shall progress through a defined state machine: created, validating, preparing, provider_pending, streaming, finalizing, reconciliation_pending, completed, failed, cancellation_requested, cancelled. A terminal execution shall not return to a non-terminal state. Late provider events shall not reactivate a terminal execution.
acceptanceCriteria: All state transitions defined and enforced
failureAndRecoveryRules: Failed terminal state persists; reconciliation separate
stateRules: Only non-terminal to terminal transitions permitted
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Workspace as Primary Ownership and Isolation Boundary
requirements.statement: Workspace shall be the primary ownership, authorization, isolation, usage, retention, export, deletion, and recovery boundary. Every workspace-scoped entity shall carry or resolve an authoritative workspace identifier at the query, cache, queue, index, and audit level.
acceptanceCriteria: All workspace-scoped queries enforce workspace filter
securityConstraints: Workspace ID shall never be user-supplied for authorization
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: API Base Path and Resource-Oriented Conventions
requirements.statement: All API endpoints shall use base path /api/v1 with versioned, resource-oriented design. All request/response bodies shall use JSON with camelCase keys, opaque string identifiers, and RFC 3339 UTC timestamps. Provider-neutral contracts shall never expose provider-native request/response shapes as public domain contracts.
securityConstraints: Credentials, prompts, memory content, file bodies never in URL or headers
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: SSE Streaming Protocol for Execution Events
requirements.statement: Execution events shall be streamed via Server-Sent Events (SSE) with text/event-stream content type. Each event carries: id, type, executionId, monotonic sequence number, occurredAt timestamp, and versioned data object. Authorization shall be revalidated on connection and reconnect. Last-Event-ID shall support bounded replay from persisted cursor.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Zero-Trust Security Model
requirements.statement: The platform shall operate on a zero-trust model assuming: untrusted clients, untrusted user content, untrusted uploaded files, untrusted retrieved text, untrusted provider output, untrusted integration payloads, and potentially compromised individual components. Optional services shall fail without weakening mandatory isolation or authorization.
acceptanceCriteria: All trust boundaries assume untrusted input
Optional service failures do not bypass mandatory controls
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Database Design Principles
requirements.statement: Every tenant-owned row shall carry workspace_id. Stable generated identifiers shall be used as primary keys; provider identifiers are attributes only. Canonical records and derived search/vector/cache/analytics records shall remain separate. Timestamps shall be UTC. Mutable aggregates shall carry a version column. Secrets shall be stored only in a secret manager; tables shall retain opaque credential references. Runtime/route/context/prompt/usage/audit evidence shall be append-only or immutable after finalization.
acceptanceCriteria: All tables follow the design conventions
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: � Input Token Count
requirements.statement: The system shall record the input-token count for each provider execution where the provider reports or the system can reliably calculate it.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Output Token Count
requirements.statement: The system shall record the output-token count for each provider execution where available.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Total Token Count
requirements.statement: The system shall calculate and record the total token count as the sum of applicable input and output token usage.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Token Estimate
requirements.statement: Where provider-reported token usage is unavailable, the system shall record a clearly identified estimate using the applicable tokenizer or conservative estimation method.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Token Source Classification
requirements.statement: Token records shall identify whether values are provider reported, locally calculated, estimated, or reconciled.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Token Attribution
requirements.statement: Token usage shall be attributed to the applicable user, workspace, project, conversation, runtime execution, provider, and model where available.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Token Reconciliation
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve initial estimates and later provider-reported usage when reconciliation changes the recorded values.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Estimated Provider Cost
requirements.statement: The system shall calculate estimated provider cost for each eligible execution.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Pricing Version
requirements.statement: Each cost calculation shall identify the pricing version or effective pricing timestamp used.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Cost Precision
requirements.statement: Cost calculations shall use sufficient numeric precision to avoid material rounding error across aggregated usage.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Display Rounding
requirements.statement: User-facing cost displays may round values for readability, but stored accounting values shall preserve the approved precision.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Failed and Cancelled Cost
requirements.statement: The system shall record provider cost for failed or cancelled executions when usage was incurred or reported.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Cost Attribution
requirements.statement: Cost records shall be attributed to the responsible workspace and user context.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Cost Aggregation
requirements.statement: The system shall aggregate usage and estimated cost over supported periods.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Usage Inspection
requirements.statement: An authorized user shall be able to inspect workspace usage by provider, model, conversation, project, and time period where the data is available.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Soft Spending Ceiling
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to configure a soft spending ceiling for the workspace.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Hard Spending Ceiling
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to configure a hard spending ceiling.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � User Soft Spending Ceiling
requirements.statement: The system shall support a soft spending ceiling attributable to an individual user where multi-user workspaces are enabled.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � User Hard Spending Ceiling
requirements.statement: The system shall support a hard spending ceiling attributable to an individual user where enabled.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Ceiling Precedence
requirements.statement: When multiple spending ceilings apply, the most restrictive effective limit shall control unless an approved policy defines otherwise.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Ceiling Time Window
requirements.statement: Each spending ceiling shall define its measurement period.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Ceiling Reset
requirements.statement: Periodic ceilings shall reset according to server-controlled time and the configured timezone or billing period.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Ceiling Concurrency Control
requirements.statement: Spending-ceiling enforcement shall account for concurrent in-flight executions.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Ceiling Validation Failure
requirements.statement: If spending-ceiling state cannot be reliably determined, the system shall fail closed for hard-ceiling enforcement or apply an explicitly approved degraded-mode policy.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Spending Warning Notification
requirements.statement: The system shall notify eligible users when usage reaches configured warning thresholds.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Usage Quota
requirements.statement: The system shall support configurable usage quotas based on request count, token count, cost, file volume, storage, or other approved units.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Quota Enforcement
requirements.statement: The system shall validate applicable quota before accepting or executing a controlled operation.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Rate Limit
requirements.statement: The system shall apply configurable rate limits to protected or resource-intensive operations.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Rate-Limit Error
requirements.statement: A rate-limited request shall return a stable error and, where safe, an indication of when retry may be attempted.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrator Authentication
requirements.statement: System Administrator access shall require an authenticated administrator identity and the applicable elevated authentication controls.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrator Authorization
requirements.statement: Every administrative action shall require an explicit administrative permission.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Least-Privilege Administration
requirements.statement: Administrative roles shall be separable by operational responsibility.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � User Account Administration
requirements.statement: An authorized System Administrator may inspect limited account status and perform approved account actions.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Administration
requirements.statement: An authorized System Administrator may perform approved workspace operational actions without receiving routine plaintext access to provider credentials or private content.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Administration
requirements.statement: An authorized System Administrator shall be able to enable, disable, or mark a provider or model as degraded at the platform level.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Feature Administration
requirements.statement: The system may allow authorized administrators to control feature availability by approved scope.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Impersonation Prohibition
requirements.statement: The system shall not permit unrestricted administrator impersonation of a user.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Audit Log
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an append-oriented audit log for administrative and security-significant actions.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Event Fields
requirements.statement: Each audit event shall include, where applicable:

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Secret Exclusion
requirements.statement: Audit events shall not contain plaintext passwords, session secrets, recovery tokens, provider API keys, access tokens, or complete private prompts unless explicitly required by an approved legal policy.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Immutability
requirements.statement: Ordinary users and administrators shall not be able to modify existing audit-event content.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Access Control
requirements.statement: Audit-log access shall require explicit permission and shall be filtered by the actor’s authorized scope.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Search
requirements.statement: Authorized users shall be able to search audit events by supported criteria.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Pagination
requirements.statement: Audit queries shall use deterministic pagination and bounded result sizes.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Retention
requirements.statement: Audit events shall be retained according to an approved retention schedule that distinguishes security, billing, operational, and user-content concerns.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Audit Export
requirements.statement: Authorized actors may export permitted audit records without exposing inaccessible workspace data or secrets.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Data Export Request
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to request export of eligible account or workspace data.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Scope
requirements.statement: A workspace export shall include eligible user-owned data within the selected scope.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Secret Exclusion
requirements.statement: Data exports shall not contain plaintext provider credentials, password hashes, session tokens, recovery tokens, encryption keys, or internal service secrets.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Data Segregation
requirements.statement: Export generation shall verify that every included record belongs to the authorized export scope.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export State
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit state for each export request.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Generation
requirements.statement: Large exports shall be generated asynchronously by an authorized background process.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Integrity
requirements.statement: The system shall verify export completeness and file integrity before marking the export ready.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Download Authorization
requirements.statement: Export downloads shall require authorization and shall use time-limited access.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Expiration
requirements.statement: Ready export artifacts shall expire and be deleted after the configured availability period.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Idempotency
requirements.statement: Retried processing of the same export request shall not create uncontrolled duplicate export artifacts.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Export Audit
requirements.statement: Export request, generation, download, expiration, and deletion shall generate applicable audit events.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Account Deletion Request
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an eligible Authenticated User to request deletion of the user account.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Deletion Request
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to request permanent deletion of an eligible workspace.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Cooling-Off Period
requirements.statement: The system may apply a configurable recovery or cooling-off period before irreversible deletion begins.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Cancellation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to cancel a deletion request before irreversible deletion begins.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Complete Erasure Processing
requirements.statement: Once irreversible deletion begins, the system shall delete or irreversibly de-identify all user or workspace data not subject to an approved legal or security retention obligation.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Erasure
requirements.statement: Complete workspace or account deletion shall remove stored provider credentials from the credential vault and invalidate cached credential material.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Derived Data Erasure
requirements.statement: Complete deletion shall remove embeddings, search indexes, cached context, generated summaries, and other derived data associated exclusively with the deleted source.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Backup Deletion Handling
requirements.statement: Data present in controlled backups shall become inaccessible to normal production use and shall expire according to the approved backup-retention schedule.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Legal Retention Exception
requirements.statement: Where data cannot be immediately deleted because of a valid legal, fraud-prevention, security, or financial-retention obligation, the system shall restrict the retained data to the minimum required scope.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Job Idempotency
requirements.statement: Account and workspace deletion jobs shall be idempotent.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Failure Reconciliation
requirements.statement: Partial deletion failure shall place the request in a controlled reconciliation state.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Completion Record
requirements.statement: The system shall retain a minimal deletion-completion record containing no prohibited user content.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Confirmation
requirements.statement: The system shall notify the eligible user when deletion completes or when a material deletion failure requires action.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Error Normalization
requirements.statement: Administrative operations shall return stable error categories consistent with the document-wide Error Convention.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Atomicity
requirements.statement: Administrative operations affecting security, provider state, spending limits, or deletion shall define and enforce atomic or recoverable state transitions.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Idempotency
requirements.statement: Administrative write operations that may be retried shall support idempotency where duplicate action could cause repeated restriction, duplicate export, duplicate deletion, or inconsistent configuration.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Time Recording
requirements.statement: Administrative state changes shall record effective time, actor, and applicable expiration time.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Administrative Notification
requirements.statement: The system shall notify affected users of material administrative actions where notification is permitted and does not compromise security.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � User Registration
requirements.statement: The system shall allow a Visitor to create a Gexor user account by submitting the mandatory registration attributes defined by the approved identity policy.

requirements.title: � Registration Email Verification
requirements.statement: The system shall require verification of the user-controlled email address before granting full authenticated access where email verification is enabled by platform policy.

requirements.title: � Registration Abuse Protection
requirements.statement: The system shall apply configurable abuse-prevention controls to account-registration attempts.

requirements.title: � Account Initialization Atomicity
requirements.statement: The system shall ensure that user identity creation and mandatory account initialization complete as one recoverable operation.

requirements.title: � Credential-Based Login
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an eligible registered user to authenticate using an approved login identifier and valid authentication credential.

requirements.title: � Authentication Failure Privacy
requirements.statement: The system shall return a generic authentication failure response that does not disclose whether the submitted account identifier exists.

requirements.title: � Login Attempt Throttling
requirements.statement: The system shall throttle repeated failed authentication attempts according to configurable security policy.

requirements.title: � Account Lock and Restriction
requirements.statement: The system shall support temporary or administrative restriction of authentication for an account.

requirements.title: � Optional Federated Authentication
requirements.statement: Where approved federated identity providers are enabled, the system may allow a Visitor to authenticate through a supported external identity provider.

requirements.title: � Password Creation
requirements.statement: Where password authentication is supported, the system shall require newly created passwords to satisfy the approved password policy.

requirements.title: � Password Storage
requirements.statement: The system shall store user passwords only as outputs of an approved one-way password-hashing function with unique salts and configured work factors.

requirements.title: � Password Change
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an Authenticated User to change the account password after satisfying the applicable re-authentication policy.

requirements.title: � Password-Reuse Control
requirements.statement: Where password-history enforcement is enabled, the system shall reject reuse of password values covered by the configured history policy.

requirements.title: � Password-Recovery Request
requirements.statement: The system shall allow a Visitor to request password recovery for an account using an approved account identifier.

requirements.title: � Password-Recovery Token
requirements.statement: The system shall generate a single-purpose, time-limited, cryptographically unpredictable token for an eligible password-recovery request.

requirements.title: � Password Reset Completion
requirements.statement: The system shall allow the user to establish a replacement password after successful validation of an active password-recovery token.

requirements.title: � Current-Session Logout
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an Authenticated User to terminate the current authenticated session.

requirements.title: � Logout From All Sessions
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an Authenticated User to terminate all active sessions associated with the account after satisfying applicable re-authentication controls.

requirements.title: � Session Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall create a unique authenticated session after successful authentication.

requirements.title: � Session Expiration
requirements.statement: The system shall enforce configurable session inactivity and absolute-lifetime limits.

requirements.title: � Session Renewal
requirements.statement: The system may renew an eligible session without requiring full authentication when the renewal request satisfies the approved session-renewal policy.

requirements.title: � Session Revocation
requirements.statement: The system shall support immediate logical revocation of a specific session.

requirements.title: � Concurrent Session Inspection
requirements.statement: The system should allow an Authenticated User to inspect active sessions associated with the account.

requirements.title: � Session Credential Protection
requirements.statement: The system shall protect session credentials against unauthorized disclosure, replay, and client-side access beyond the requirements of the supported client architecture.

requirements.title: � Sensitive-Action Re-authentication
requirements.statement: The system shall require recent authentication or an approved additional verification step before completing designated sensitive operations.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Submission
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to submit a supported AI-provider credential through an approved protected interface.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Encryption
requirements.statement: The system shall encrypt each stored AI-provider credential using an approved secrets-management or encryption mechanism before durable persistence.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Vaulting
requirements.statement: The system should store provider credentials in a dedicated secrets vault or equivalent controlled credential store.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Masking
requirements.statement: The system shall display stored provider credentials only in masked, non-recoverable form.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Access Control
requirements.statement: The system shall restrict provider-credential creation, replacement, validation, disabling, and deletion to explicitly authorized actors.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Replacement
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to replace an existing provider credential without exposing the previous credential.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Deletion
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to delete or disconnect a stored provider credential.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Logging Prohibition
requirements.statement: The system shall not write complete provider API keys, access tokens, refresh tokens, client secrets, or equivalent reusable credentials to logs, traces, analytics, audit records, exception messages, or user-visible errors.

requirements.title: � Credential-Rotation Support
requirements.statement: The system shall support replacement and rotation of provider credentials without requiring deletion of the associated workspace, conversations, memories, or usage history.

requirements.title: � Identity Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall generate audit events for security-significant identity operations.

requirements.title: � Background Job Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall create an explicit background-job record for each asynchronous processing activity that can affect persistent state, retrieval eligibility, usage reconciliation, notification delivery, export, or deletion.

requirements.title: � Background Job State Model
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit lifecycle state for every background job.

requirements.title: � Workspace Scope Validation
requirements.statement: A Background Processing Worker shall validate the immutable workspace scope and target eligibility before reading or modifying protected data.

requirements.title: � Background Job Idempotency
requirements.statement: A retried or redelivered background job shall not create duplicate active memories, knowledge records, file segments, embeddings, usage records, exports, notifications, or deletion effects.

requirements.title: � Background Job Claiming
requirements.statement: The system shall prevent two workers from concurrently applying the same indivisible job effect unless the job type explicitly supports coordinated parallel processing.

requirements.title: � Retry Classification
requirements.statement: Background-processing failures shall be classified as retryable, non-retryable, cancelled, or requiring reconciliation.

requirements.title: � Stale Job Detection
requirements.statement: A Scheduled System Process shall identify jobs that remain running or claimed beyond the configured processing lease or timeout.

requirements.title: � Job Cancellation
requirements.statement: The system shall support cancellation or invalidation of eligible queued or running background jobs when their source is deleted, their workspace becomes ineligible, or an authorized operation requires cancellation.

requirements.title: � Source Snapshot
requirements.statement: Each background job shall process an identifiable source version or immutable source snapshot sufficient to prevent an older job from overwriting a newer committed state.

requirements.title: � Late Result Rejection
requirements.statement: The system shall reject or quarantine a background result when its source, workspace, target version, deletion state, or authorization condition no longer permits application.

requirements.title: � Background Failure Isolation
requirements.statement: Failure or backlog in one background-job type or workspace shall not block unrelated synchronous chat execution except where a mandatory safety, deletion, quota, or integrity dependency requires blocking.

requirements.title: � Background Processing Audit
requirements.statement: The system shall retain operationally sufficient job history for security-significant, billing-significant, export, deletion, memory, and knowledge processing.

requirements.title: � Project Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Authenticated User to create a project within an active workspace.

requirements.title: � Project Stable Identifier
requirements.statement: Each project shall receive a stable identifier that shall not change when the project is renamed, archived, restored, or moved between permitted lifecycle states.

requirements.title: � Project Update
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to update supported project attributes.

requirements.title: � Project Archive
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to archive an active project.

requirements.title: � Project Restore
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to restore an archived project when the containing workspace is active.

requirements.title: � Project Deletion
requirements.statement: The system shall support soft deletion and controlled permanent deletion of a project.

requirements.title: � Conversation Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Authenticated User to create a conversation within an active workspace and, where applicable, an active project.

requirements.title: � Conversation-Creation Idempotency
requirements.statement: The conversation-creation operation should support an idempotency key to prevent duplicate conversations caused by retries.

requirements.title: � Conversation Default Title
requirements.statement: The system shall assign a default title when a conversation is created without an explicit valid title.

requirements.title: � Conversation Naming
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to assign or update the conversation title.

requirements.title: � Conversation Metadata
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain metadata for each conversation.

requirements.title: � Conversation Retrieval
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to retrieve an accessible conversation and its permitted metadata.

requirements.title: � Conversation Listing
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to list conversations within the selected workspace or project.

requirements.title: � Conversation Pagination
requirements.statement: Conversation lists shall use deterministic pagination.

requirements.title: � Conversation Attribute Update
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to update supported mutable conversation attributes.

requirements.title: � Conversation Project Reassignment
requirements.statement: Where project reassignment is supported, the system shall allow an authorized actor to move a conversation between eligible projects within the same workspace.

requirements.title: � Conversation Runtime Preference
requirements.statement: The system may allow conversation-specific runtime settings that override eligible workspace or project defaults.

requirements.title: � Conversation Archive
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to archive an active conversation.

requirements.title: � Conversation Restore
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to restore an archived conversation when its workspace and project are eligible.

requirements.title: � Conversation Soft Delete
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to soft-delete a conversation.

requirements.title: � Soft-Delete Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated soft-delete requests for the same already soft-deleted conversation shall succeed idempotently without creating duplicate deletion records.

requirements.title: � Conversation Recovery
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to restore a soft-deleted conversation during the configured recovery period.

requirements.title: � Permanent Conversation Deletion Request
requirements.statement: The system shall require explicit authorized confirmation before permanently deleting a conversation.

requirements.title: � Permanent Conversation Deletion Processing
requirements.statement: The system shall permanently delete or irreversibly de-identify the conversation and its exclusively owned dependent content according to the approved deletion policy.

requirements.title: � Permanent Deletion Integrity
requirements.statement: The system shall verify that permanent conversation deletion does not remove shared records still required by another authorized resource.

requirements.title: � Permanent Deletion State
requirements.statement: A conversation accepted for permanent deletion shall enter a deletion-pending state until all required deletion steps complete.

requirements.title: � Conversation Ordering
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain deterministic message ordering within a conversation using server-controlled sequence or timestamp data.

requirements.title: � Conversation Activity Time
requirements.statement: The system shall update the conversation’s last-activity time after accepted user messages and completed system events that qualify as activity.

requirements.title: � Conversation Exclusion From Memory
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to mark a conversation as excluded from future memory extraction where the workspace policy permits such control.

requirements.title: � Conversation Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit conversation creation, project reassignment, archive, restore, soft deletion, recovery, permanent-deletion request, and permanent-deletion completion.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Record
requirements.statement: The system shall represent retained knowledge as a structured or indexed record associated with an authorized workspace and an identifiable source.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Source Eligibility
requirements.statement: The system shall create knowledge records only from eligible user input, conversations, files, or other sources authorized by workspace policy.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Extraction
requirements.statement: The Background Processing Worker shall extract eligible knowledge outside the critical response path where synchronous extraction is not required.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Deduplication
requirements.statement: Before activating newly extracted knowledge, the system shall evaluate materially equivalent active knowledge within the authorized scope.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Retrieval
requirements.statement: Runtime knowledge retrieval shall return only active, permitted, source-valid, and contextually relevant knowledge records.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Provenance
requirements.statement: Each knowledge record shall preserve sufficient provenance to identify the source material and processing activity from which it was derived.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Update and Reprocessing
requirements.statement: A material source update shall cause affected knowledge to be invalidated, versioned, or scheduled for reprocessing according to the approved source-change policy.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Deletion Reconciliation
requirements.statement: When a knowledge source is permanently deleted, the system shall delete, de-identify, invalidate, or re-evaluate knowledge derived from that source according to provenance and retention policy.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Processing Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated processing of the same source version under the same processing purpose shall not create duplicate active knowledge, segments, embeddings, or index entries.

requirements.title: � Knowledge Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit security-significant knowledge activation, reprocessing, invalidation, restoration, and permanent deletion.

requirements.title: � Structured Memory Record
requirements.statement: The system shall represent retained memory as a structured record rather than relying exclusively on raw conversation history.

requirements.title: � Memory Workspace Binding
requirements.statement: Every memory shall be bound to exactly one workspace.

requirements.title: � Memory Scope
requirements.statement: The system shall support memory scope sufficient to distinguish workspace, project, conversation, user-preference, and temporary-context applicability where enabled.

requirements.title: � Memory Categories
requirements.statement: The system shall classify memories using approved categories.

requirements.title: � Background Memory Extraction
requirements.statement: The Background Processing Worker shall evaluate eligible completed conversations or messages for potential long-term memory after or outside the critical response path.

requirements.title: � Extraction Eligibility
requirements.statement: The system shall determine memory-extraction eligibility before processing source content.

requirements.title: � Memory Candidate Creation
requirements.statement: Extracted information shall first be created as a memory candidate unless policy explicitly permits automatic confirmation.

requirements.title: � Candidate Deduplication
requirements.statement: The system shall compare a memory candidate against existing eligible memories before creating a new active memory.

requirements.title: � Candidate Conflict Detection
requirements.statement: The system shall detect material conflicts between a candidate and existing active memory where feasible.

requirements.title: � Automatic Memory Policy
requirements.statement: Automatic memory activation shall occur only for categories, confidence levels, and workspace modes explicitly permitted by policy.

requirements.title: � Memory Extraction Failure
requirements.statement: Memory-extraction failure shall not change the completed user-visible response state.

requirements.title: � User Confirmation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to confirm an eligible memory candidate.

requirements.title: � Memory Rejection
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to reject a memory candidate.

requirements.title: � Memory Activation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to activate an inactive memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Deactivation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to deactivate an active memory without deleting the memory record.

requirements.title: � Relevant Memory Retrieval
requirements.statement: The system shall retrieve only active, permitted, and contextually relevant memories for a runtime execution.

requirements.title: � Workspace Scope Enforcement
requirements.statement: Every memory-retrieval operation shall include and enforce the authorized workspace scope.

requirements.title: � Project Scope Enforcement
requirements.statement: Project-scoped memories shall be retrieved only when the active execution is associated with the same project or an explicitly authorized related scope.

requirements.title: � Conversation Exclusion Enforcement
requirements.statement: The system shall not retrieve memory into a conversation where applicable conversation or workspace settings prohibit memory use.

requirements.title: � Memory Relevance Ranking
requirements.statement: Eligible memories shall be ranked using approved relevance signals.

requirements.title: � Retrieval Threshold
requirements.statement: A memory shall not be included in runtime context when its retrieval score or policy eligibility falls below the configured threshold.

requirements.title: � Retrieval Limit
requirements.statement: The system shall limit retrieved memories by count, token budget, or both.

requirements.title: � Retrieval Deduplication
requirements.statement: The system shall suppress materially duplicate retrieved memories before context construction.

requirements.title: � Conflict-Aware Retrieval
requirements.statement: Memories in unresolved conflict shall not be presented as unqualified confirmed facts.

requirements.title: � Memory Retrieval Snapshot
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve the identifiers and effective versions of memories selected for each runtime execution.

requirements.title: � Memory Retrieval Failure
requirements.statement: If memory retrieval fails, the Runtime Orchestrator shall apply the configured failure policy.

requirements.title: � Memory Listing
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to list memories within the selected workspace.

requirements.title: � Memory Detail Inspection
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to inspect an individual memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Provenance
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve the provenance of each extracted memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Usage Inspection
requirements.statement: The system should allow an authorized user to determine whether a memory was used in a specific runtime execution.

requirements.title: � Memory Content Update
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to update the content of an eligible memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Category Update
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to change the category or scope of an eligible memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Versioning
requirements.statement: Material memory updates shall preserve prior version metadata sufficient for audit and conflict analysis.

requirements.title: � Memory Update Atomicity
requirements.statement: Memory content, version metadata, and retrieval-index transition shall complete as one recoverable operation.

requirements.title: � Memory Soft Delete
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to soft-delete a memory.

requirements.title: � Memory Restore
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to restore a soft-deleted memory before permanent deletion begins.

requirements.title: � Permanent Memory Deletion
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to permanently delete an eligible memory after explicit confirmation.

requirements.title: � Memory Vector Deletion
requirements.statement: Permanent deletion or deactivation of a memory shall remove or disable all vector representations used for retrieval.

requirements.title: � Memory Keyword Index Deletion
requirements.statement: Permanent deletion or deactivation of a memory shall remove or disable associated keyword-search entries.

requirements.title: � Memory Deletion Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated deletion processing for the same memory and deletion request shall be idempotent.

requirements.title: � Source Deletion Reconciliation
requirements.statement: When a source conversation, message, or file is permanently deleted, the system shall evaluate memories derived from that source.

requirements.title: � Temporary Context Creation
requirements.statement: The system may create temporary context records that are eligible for limited use without becoming long-term active memory.

requirements.title: � Temporary Context Expiration
requirements.statement: Temporary context shall have an explicit expiration condition.

requirements.title: � Temporary Context Cleanup
requirements.statement: Expired temporary context shall be removed from retrieval and processed for deletion according to retention policy.

requirements.title: � Memory Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit memory confirmation, rejection, activation, deactivation, content update, scope update, conflict resolution, soft deletion, restoration, and permanent deletion.

requirements.title: � User Message Submission
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Authenticated User to submit a message to an eligible active conversation.

requirements.title: � Message Stable Identifier
requirements.statement: Each accepted message shall receive a unique stable identifier before or at the start of runtime processing.

requirements.title: � Message Workspace Binding
requirements.statement: Each message shall be permanently bound to exactly one workspace and one conversation.

requirements.title: � Message Project Binding
requirements.statement: Each message shall record the effective project context, where applicable, at the time of submission.

requirements.title: � Message Idempotency
requirements.statement: Message submission shall support an idempotency key to prevent duplicate user messages and duplicate provider executions caused by client retries.

requirements.title: � Supported Message Content
requirements.statement: The system shall accept supported textual message content and approved attachment references.

requirements.title: � Message Content Preservation
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve the user-submitted message content as the canonical user input, subject only to explicit normalization required for safe storage.

requirements.title: � Message Edit Policy
requirements.statement: Where message editing is supported, the system shall preserve the original message version or equivalent audit history.

requirements.title: � Message State Lifecycle
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit state for every user message and generated assistant message.

requirements.title: � Accepted State
requirements.statement: A user message shall enter the accepted state after validation and durable creation succeed.

requirements.title: � Sending State
requirements.statement: A message execution shall enter the sending state when the Runtime Orchestrator begins provider-request preparation or transmission.

requirements.title: � Streaming State
requirements.statement: The assistant message shall enter the streaming state when the system receives or emits the first valid response event associated with the provider generation.

requirements.title: � Complete State
requirements.statement: The assistant message shall enter the complete state only after the provider response stream or non-streamed response has ended successfully and the final response content has been durably recorded.

requirements.title: � Failed State
requirements.statement: A message execution shall enter the failed state when the system cannot complete the provider interaction and no valid fallback completes the request.

requirements.title: � Cancelled State
requirements.statement: A message execution shall enter the cancelled state when an authorized cancellation request is accepted and no further normal generation output will be processed.

requirements.title: � Terminal State Immutability
requirements.statement: Complete, failed, and cancelled message states shall be terminal for the associated execution.

requirements.title: � Assistant Message Placeholder
requirements.statement: The system shall create or reserve an assistant-message record before streaming response content where required to preserve ordering and recovery.

requirements.title: � Streamed Content Persistence
requirements.statement: The system shall persist streamed assistant content using a strategy that supports recovery from service interruption without producing invalid message ordering.

requirements.title: � Partial Response Visibility
requirements.statement: The system may display partial assistant content during streaming.

requirements.title: � Active Generation Cancellation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to request cancellation of an active generation associated with the current conversation.

requirements.title: � Cancellation Propagation
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall attempt to propagate cancellation to the active provider request where the provider supports cancellation.

requirements.title: � Cancellation Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated cancellation requests for the same execution shall be idempotent.

requirements.title: � Cancellation Billing Metadata
requirements.statement: The system shall record any provider usage reported for a cancelled request and shall distinguish estimated cost from confirmed provider-reported cost.

requirements.title: � Message Actor Metadata
requirements.statement: Each message shall identify its role or actor classification.

requirements.title: � Message Time Metadata
requirements.statement: Each message shall record server-controlled creation time and applicable state-transition times.

requirements.title: � Runtime Execution Binding
requirements.statement: Every provider-generated assistant message shall be bound to the runtime execution that produced it.

requirements.title: � Provider Metadata Binding
requirements.statement: Each completed or failed provider execution shall record the effective provider and model identifiers used.

requirements.title: � Context Metadata Binding
requirements.statement: Each runtime execution shall preserve metadata identifying the context categories used to produce the provider request.

requirements.title: � Usage Metadata Binding
requirements.statement: Each runtime execution shall record input-token, output-token, total-token, and estimated-cost information where measurable.

requirements.title: � Error Metadata Binding
requirements.statement: Failed messages shall record a stable user-safe error code and an internal correlation identifier.

requirements.title: � Failed Message Retry
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to retry an eligible failed execution.

requirements.title: � Response Regeneration
requirements.statement: The system may allow an authorized user to regenerate an assistant response for an existing user message.

requirements.title: � Message Deletion
requirements.statement: Where individual message deletion is supported, the system shall process deletion without corrupting conversation ordering, memory provenance, or retained execution records.

requirements.title: � Message Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit security-sensitive message actions, including cancellation, administrative deletion, permanent deletion, and policy-driven content restriction.

requirements.title: � Notification Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall create a notification only when an approved functional event and notification policy require or permit delivery.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Authorization
requirements.statement: Before delivery, the Notification Service shall verify that the intended recipient remains authorized to receive information about the related resource or event.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Preference Enforcement
requirements.statement: The Notification Service shall apply effective user and workspace notification preferences except where a mandatory security, legal, billing, or destructive-action notice cannot be disabled.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Sensitive Content Minimization
requirements.statement: Notifications delivered through channels not approved for sensitive content shall contain only the minimum information necessary to communicate the event and a protected path to further details.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Delivery State
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit delivery state for each notification attempt.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Retry
requirements.statement: Eligible transient notification failures shall be retried according to a bounded retry policy.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Idempotency
requirements.statement: Notification creation and delivery shall support idempotency where repeated event processing could otherwise deliver duplicate security, billing, export, or deletion notices.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Notification Audit
requirements.statement: The system shall record delivery metadata for security-significant, billing-significant, export, restriction, and deletion notifications without recording prohibited secret content.

source.section: 13. Usage, Billing, Notification, and Administration Requirements

requirements.title: � Supported Provider Registry
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain a registry of supported AI providers and their applicable capabilities.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � BYOK Connection Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to create a Bring Your Own Key provider connection for a supported provider.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Connection Identifier
requirements.statement: Each provider connection shall receive a stable identifier independent of the secret value.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Connection State
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit state for each provider connection.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Validation
requirements.statement: The system shall validate a newly submitted provider credential through an approved provider-specific validation mechanism where available.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Connection Health Check
requirements.statement: The system shall perform health checks for active provider connections according to configurable policy.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Health Check Isolation
requirements.statement: Provider health checks shall use the target workspace’s connection and shall not reuse another workspace’s credential.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Health Check Result
requirements.statement: The system shall record the latest provider-connection health state and check time.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Manual Health Check
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to request a provider-connection health check subject to rate limits.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Connection Disable
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to disable a provider connection without deleting its historical usage records.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Disconnection
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to disconnect a provider and delete its stored credential.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Model Catalogue
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain a catalogue of models available through each supported provider.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Model Catalogue Refresh
requirements.statement: The system shall support refresh of provider model metadata without rewriting historical execution records.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Default Provider
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to select an active provider connection as the workspace default.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Workspace Default Model
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to select an eligible model as the workspace default.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Project Default Model
requirements.statement: The system may allow an authorized user to configure a project-level default model that overrides the workspace default when permitted.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Conversation Model Override
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to select an eligible provider or model for a conversation or individual execution when permitted by policy.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Effective Model Resolution
requirements.statement: The system shall resolve the effective model using the approved configuration-precedence order.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Model Capability Validation
requirements.statement: Before provider execution, the system shall verify that the selected model supports the required request capabilities.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Model Unavailability
requirements.statement: If the selected model is unavailable, the system shall apply the configured unavailability policy.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Routing Decision
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall select a provider connection and model based on explicit user selection or approved dynamic-routing policy.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � User-Selected Provider Priority
requirements.statement: An explicit user-selected provider or model shall take precedence over dynamic recommendation unless the selection is invalid, unavailable, prohibited, or exceeds mandatory limits.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Cost-Aware Routing
requirements.statement: Dynamic routing may select a lower-cost eligible model when it satisfies the required capability and quality policy.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Capability-Aware Routing
requirements.statement: The system shall not route a request to a model that lacks a mandatory task capability.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Routing Transparency
requirements.statement: The execution record shall preserve the routing reason category and selected provider and model.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Policy
requirements.statement: The system shall support configurable fallback routing when the primary provider or model cannot complete an eligible request.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Permission
requirements.statement: Fallback shall occur only when permitted by workspace policy and applicable user selection.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Model Eligibility
requirements.statement: A fallback model shall satisfy the mandatory request capabilities, context requirement, security policy, and spending ceiling.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Cost Validation
requirements.statement: The system shall re-evaluate estimated cost before dispatching a fallback request.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Context Revalidation
requirements.statement: The system shall revalidate and, where necessary, reconstruct the provider request for the fallback model’s context and formatting requirements.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Attempt Limit
requirements.statement: The system shall enforce a configurable maximum number of fallback attempts per runtime execution.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Duplicate Provider Execution Prevention
requirements.statement: The system shall not initiate a fallback while the primary provider may still produce a valid response unless the primary request has been cancelled, timed out, or placed in a state that prevents duplicate response acceptance.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Result Attribution
requirements.statement: The final assistant message shall identify the provider and model that actually produced the accepted response.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Fallback Failure
requirements.statement: If all eligible fallback attempts fail, the execution shall enter a failed state with a stable aggregated failure category.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Error Normalization
requirements.statement: The system shall map provider-specific failures into stable internal error categories.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Error Privacy
requirements.statement: Provider errors exposed to users shall not include stored credentials, request signatures, internal endpoints, or another account’s information.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Usage Capture
requirements.statement: The system shall capture provider-reported usage and request identifiers where available.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Pricing Association
requirements.statement: The system shall associate provider usage with the effective model-pricing version used for cost calculation.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Provider Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit provider-connection creation, credential validation, default selection, disabling, disconnection, routing fallback, and administrative provider-state changes.

source.section: 11. AI Provider Connection and Routing Requirements

requirements.title: � Runtime Execution Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall create a unique runtime execution record for each accepted request that may invoke an AI provider.

requirements.title: � Runtime Eligibility Validation
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall validate execution eligibility before sending a request to an AI provider.

requirements.title: � Runtime Execution State
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit state for each runtime execution.

requirements.title: � Controlled Pipeline Order
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall process each eligible request through a controlled pipeline.

requirements.title: � Pipeline Stage Traceability
requirements.statement: The system shall record the status and failure category of each material runtime stage.

requirements.title: � Pipeline Failure Isolation
requirements.statement: Failure in one runtime execution shall not modify or block unrelated executions outside the shared resource limits defined by platform policy.

requirements.title: � Intent Classification
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall classify the user request into an approved intent or task category before provider-ready prompt construction where classification is required.

requirements.title: � Classification Confidence
requirements.statement: Where classification produces a confidence score or equivalent certainty state, the system shall apply a configured fallback when confidence is insufficient.

requirements.title: � Classification Failure
requirements.statement: Intent-classification failure shall not automatically fail the request when a safe general execution path is available.

requirements.title: � Classification Preservation
requirements.statement: The execution record shall preserve the effective classification used for routing and prompt construction.

requirements.title: � Execution Route Resolution
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall select an execution route based on the effective request, policy, provider availability, model capability, context requirement, cost control, and user configuration.

requirements.title: � Direct Route
requirements.statement: The system shall support a direct route for eligible requests that do not require memory retrieval, file processing, complex prompt enhancement, or special model capabilities.

requirements.title: � Enhanced Route
requirements.statement: The system shall support an enhanced route that may include intent classification, memory retrieval, context assembly, prompt enhancement, and model recommendation.

requirements.title: � Retrieval Route
requirements.statement: The system shall support a retrieval-assisted route when the request requires workspace memory, project knowledge, file content, or indexed information.

requirements.title: � Route Policy Enforcement
requirements.statement: Dynamic routing shall not bypass user-disabled features or mandatory platform restrictions.

requirements.title: � Route Transparency
requirements.statement: The system should expose a user-readable summary of the effective route, provider, model, and context categories used for a completed execution.

requirements.title: � Context Token Budget
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall calculate an effective context-token budget before constructing the provider request.

requirements.title: � Model Limit Enforcement
requirements.statement: The system shall not knowingly submit a provider request that exceeds the selected model’s supported context bound.

requirements.title: � Token Allocation Priority
requirements.statement: When available context exceeds the effective budget, the system shall reduce context according to a deterministic priority policy.

requirements.title: � Context Truncation Transparency
requirements.statement: The system shall record whether context was truncated, omitted, summarized, or compressed to satisfy the token budget.

requirements.title: � Context Deduplication
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall remove or reduce materially duplicate context before provider submission where doing so does not alter required meaning.

requirements.title: � Reserved Output Capacity
requirements.statement: The system shall reserve sufficient output capacity according to model limits, user settings, and task requirements before finalizing the input context.

requirements.title: � Token Estimation Failure
requirements.statement: If exact token estimation is unavailable, the system shall use a documented conservative estimation method.

requirements.title: � Provider-Ready Prompt Construction
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall construct a provider-ready request from the canonical user message and the approved effective context.

requirements.title: � User Intent Preservation
requirements.statement: Prompt enhancement shall preserve the user’s intended task and shall not silently introduce a materially different objective.

requirements.title: � Instruction Precedence
requirements.statement: Prompt construction shall apply a deterministic instruction-precedence model.

requirements.title: � Untrusted Context Delimitation
