requirements.statement: Retrieved memories, files, search results, and provider-returned content shall be identified as contextual data rather than trusted system instructions unless an approved policy states otherwise.

requirements.title: � Prompt Snapshot
requirements.statement: The system shall preserve an immutable or reproducible representation of the effective provider request for traceability, subject to security, privacy, and retention policy.

requirements.title: � Pre-Execution Cost Estimate
requirements.statement: The system shall calculate a pre-execution estimated provider cost where model pricing and token estimates are available.

requirements.title: � Spending-Ceiling Validation
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall validate the estimated request cost against applicable user and workspace spending ceilings before provider execution.

requirements.title: � Cost Estimate Unavailability
requirements.statement: Where reliable cost estimation is unavailable, the system shall apply the configured unknown-cost policy.

requirements.title: � Actual Usage Capture
requirements.statement: The system shall capture provider-reported token usage after completion where the provider supplies usage data.

requirements.title: � Usage Reconciliation
requirements.statement: The system shall reconcile estimated usage with provider-reported usage and shall preserve both values when they differ.

requirements.title: � Cost Calculation Versioning
requirements.statement: Each cost record shall identify the pricing data or calculation version used.

requirements.title: � Failed Execution Cost
requirements.statement: The system shall record reported or estimated provider cost for failed and cancelled executions where provider usage may have occurred.

requirements.title: � Provider Request Dispatch
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall dispatch the provider request only after all mandatory validations succeed.

requirements.title: � Execution Correlation
requirements.statement: Each provider request shall be associated with the corresponding runtime execution through an internal correlation identifier.

requirements.title: � Provider Timeout
requirements.statement: The system shall apply configurable connection, first-response, stream-idle, and total-execution timeouts.

requirements.title: � Runtime Cancellation
requirements.statement: The Runtime Orchestrator shall stop local processing after cancellation is accepted and shall prevent additional provider output from being presented as active generation.

requirements.title: � Successful Finalization
requirements.statement: A runtime execution shall be marked completed only after the final assistant content and required usage metadata have been durably recorded or placed in a recoverable reconciliation state.

requirements.title: � Failure Finalization
requirements.statement: A failed runtime execution shall record the terminal stage, error category, retry eligibility, and finalization time.

requirements.title: � Background Work Scheduling
requirements.statement: After synchronous response completion or eligible terminal failure, the Runtime Orchestrator shall schedule applicable background processing without blocking normal response delivery.

requirements.title: � Snapshot Lock for Rapid-Fire Messages
requirements.statement: If a new user message is accepted while background processing for the immediately preceding message remains active, the Runtime Orchestrator shall not block the new message waiting for that background processing to complete.

requirements.title: � Runtime Idempotent Finalization
requirements.statement: Retried completion or failure-finalization operations shall not duplicate assistant messages, usage records, cost records, or background jobs.

requirements.title: � Runtime Audit Record
requirements.statement: The system shall retain a traceable runtime record containing the effective provider, model, route, context categories, token metrics, cost metrics, state transitions, and error category.

requirements.title: � SSE Response Channel
requirements.statement: The system shall support Server-Sent Events for streaming eligible runtime response events to supported clients.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � SSE Authentication
requirements.statement: The system shall authenticate and authorize an SSE connection before transmitting workspace-scoped response data.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � SSE Content Type
requirements.statement: The system shall return the applicable SSE media type and shall format each event according to the supported event contract.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Event Types
requirements.statement: The SSE stream shall support stable event types sufficient to represent runtime progress.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Correlation
requirements.statement: Each SSE event shall identify the applicable runtime execution and message context without exposing secret identifiers.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Event Ordering
requirements.statement: Events within one execution stream shall be emitted in deterministic sequence order.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Heartbeat
requirements.statement: The system shall emit heartbeat events or equivalent keep-alive data at a configured interval during eligible periods of stream inactivity.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � First-Event Timeout
requirements.statement: The system shall apply a configurable maximum time between provider dispatch and the first valid response event.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Idle Timeout
requirements.statement: The system shall terminate or fail a stream that remains inactive beyond the configured idle limit, subject to provider-specific policy.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Client Disconnect
requirements.statement: The system shall detect client disconnection where supported.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Reconnection
requirements.statement: The system should allow an authorized client to reconnect to an active or recently completed stream.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Completion
requirements.statement: The system shall emit a terminal completion event only after the assistant response has been finalized according to FR-RUNTIME-040.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Failure
requirements.statement: Stream failure shall emit or persist a stable user-safe error state.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Stream Cancellation
requirements.statement: Accepted user cancellation shall result in a terminal cancellation event for connected clients.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � SSE Cross-Tenant Isolation
requirements.statement: The system shall not emit an event from one workspace, conversation, message, or execution to a subscriber authorized only for another scope.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Upload
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to upload supported files to an active workspace or project.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Supported MVP File Types
requirements.statement: The MVP shall support text extraction from approved PDF and plain-text file formats.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Type Verification
requirements.statement: The system shall verify file type using content inspection or equivalent trusted detection and shall not rely solely on the client-provided file extension or media type.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Size Bound
requirements.statement: The system shall reject files exceeding the configured maximum size before initiating unbounded parsing.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Stable Identifier
requirements.statement: Each accepted file shall receive a stable identifier and shall be bound to exactly one workspace.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Integrity Metadata
requirements.statement: The system shall record integrity metadata sufficient to detect accidental duplication or corruption.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Duplicate File Handling
requirements.statement: The system shall apply a documented duplicate-file policy.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Upload State
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit file-processing state.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � TXT Parsing
requirements.statement: The system shall extract supported textual content from a valid plain-text file using a supported character encoding.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � PDF Parsing
requirements.statement: The system shall extract machine-readable text from supported PDF files within configured page, size, and processing-time bounds.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Scanned PDF Handling
requirements.statement: Where OCR is not enabled, the system shall identify PDFs with insufficient extractable text and shall not falsely represent them as fully parsed.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Parsing Bounds
requirements.statement: File parsing shall enforce configurable resource bounds.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Parser Isolation
requirements.statement: File parsing shall execute in a controlled environment that prevents file content from obtaining unauthorized system or workspace access.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Content Segmentation
requirements.statement: Parsed file content shall be segmented into retrieval units suitable for keyword and vector indexing.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Parsing Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated parsing of the same file version using the same parser version shall not create duplicate active segments or duplicate index entries.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Processing Failure
requirements.statement: A file-processing failure shall place the file in a failed state with a stable error category.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Selection for Runtime
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user or approved retrieval process to select eligible files for runtime context.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Workspace Validation
requirements.statement: A selected file shall be injected only when it belongs to the active workspace and the actor has access.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Parsing Requirement
requirements.statement: A file shall not be injected as parsed context unless its required processing state is complete or explicitly marked as partial and permitted.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Context Relevance
requirements.statement: When a selected file exceeds the available context budget, the system shall select the most relevant eligible segments rather than injecting the entire file by default.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Context Token Bound
requirements.statement: File-derived context shall remain within the allocation assigned by the Runtime Orchestrator.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Source Attribution
requirements.statement: The runtime record shall preserve the file and segment identifiers used in the provider request.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Instruction Isolation
requirements.statement: Parsed file content shall be treated as untrusted contextual data and shall not override higher-priority system, workspace, project, conversation, or user instructions.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Vector Index Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall create vector embeddings for eligible memory and file segments when vector search is enabled.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Embedding Model Version
requirements.statement: Each vector record shall identify the embedding model and processing version used.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Vector Workspace Scope
requirements.statement: Every vector-search query shall enforce the authorized workspace scope.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Vector Search Query
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized runtime or user search to retrieve semantically relevant eligible records.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Vector Result Validation
requirements.statement: The system shall validate every returned vector result against workspace, lifecycle, permission, and source-availability rules before use.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Deleted Vector Exclusion
requirements.statement: Deleted, inactive, expired, or unauthorized vector records shall not be returned as eligible search results.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Vector Search Failure
requirements.statement: Vector-search failure shall not cause the system to return unscoped or stale results.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Keyword Index Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall index eligible textual content for keyword or full-text search where keyword search is enabled.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Keyword Search Query
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized actor to search eligible workspace content using supported keyword criteria.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Keyword Scope Filtering
requirements.statement: Keyword search shall enforce workspace, project, content-type, lifecycle-state, and permission filters before returning results.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Search Result Metadata
requirements.statement: Search results shall include sufficient source metadata to identify the matching resource without exposing inaccessible content.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Search Pagination
requirements.statement: User-facing search results shall support deterministic pagination or bounded top-result retrieval.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Hybrid Search
requirements.statement: The system may combine vector relevance, keyword relevance, recency, scope, and content priority into a hybrid result ranking.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Hybrid Search Determinism
requirements.statement: The effective search-ranking configuration or version shall be identifiable for traceability.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Search Deduplication
requirements.statement: The system shall remove materially duplicate search results before runtime context injection.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Search Result Limit
requirements.statement: The system shall enforce configurable result-count and token limits for all runtime search operations.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Search Audit Metadata
requirements.statement: The runtime record shall preserve the identifiers of search results selected for context injection.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Soft Delete
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized user to soft-delete a file.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Permanent Delete
requirements.statement: Permanent file deletion shall remove the file content, parsed segments, vector embeddings, keyword indexes, derived caches, and temporary download artifacts.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Deletion Idempotency
requirements.statement: Repeated file-deletion processing shall be idempotent.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � File Audit Events
requirements.statement: The system shall audit file upload, parsing failure, indexing, soft deletion, restoration, and permanent deletion where applicable.

source.section: 12. Streaming, File Processing, and Search Requirements

requirements.title: � Organization Record Support
requirements.statement: The system shall support an organization scope capable of containing one or more workspaces, users, roles, provider policies, and governance settings where organization functionality is enabled.

requirements.title: � Personal Organization Context
requirements.statement: Where the platform architecture requires an organization boundary for all workspaces, the system shall create or assign a personal organization context for each eligible user without exposing unnecessary organizational complexity in the MVP user experience.

requirements.title: � Organization Isolation
requirements.statement: The system shall prevent a user, service, query, job, cache entry, search result, export, or administrative operation from accessing another organization’s protected data without explicit authorized cross-organization capability.

requirements.title: � Personal Workspace Creation
requirements.statement: The system shall provision one personal workspace for each newly eligible user account.

requirements.title: � Workspace Provisioning Idempotency
requirements.statement: Personal workspace provisioning shall be idempotent for a given user and provisioning purpose.

requirements.title: � Workspace Provisioning Recovery
requirements.statement: The system shall support recovery from interrupted workspace provisioning.

requirements.title: � Default Workspace Naming
requirements.statement: The system shall assign a default display name to a newly provisioned personal workspace and shall allow the Workspace Owner to replace it with a valid name.

requirements.title: � Workspace State Model
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an explicit workspace lifecycle state.

requirements.title: � Active Workspace Access
requirements.statement: The system shall permit normal workspace operations only when the workspace is active and the actor has the required permission.

requirements.title: � Restricted Workspace Behaviour
requirements.statement: The system shall prevent prohibited operations within a restricted workspace while preserving access to any actions explicitly allowed by the restriction policy.

requirements.title: � Workspace Setting Update
requirements.statement: The system shall allow an authorized Workspace Owner to update supported workspace settings.

requirements.title: � Workspace Deactivation
requirements.statement: The system may support deactivation of a workspace without immediately deleting its data.

requirements.title: � Workspace Ownership Binding
requirements.statement: Every workspace shall have at least one valid ownership or administrative authority record unless the workspace is in a terminal deleted state.

requirements.title: � Workspace Membership Verification
requirements.statement: The system shall verify active workspace membership or ownership before authorizing any workspace-scoped operation.

requirements.title: � Workspace Context Selection
requirements.statement: The system shall require an explicit workspace context for each workspace-scoped operation.

requirements.title: � Workspace Ownership Transfer
requirements.statement: Where ownership transfer is enabled, the system shall transfer workspace ownership only through an explicit, authorized, and auditable workflow.

requirements.title: � Workspace Data Ownership
requirements.statement: Every workspace-scoped record shall be associated with a stable workspace identifier.

requirements.title: � Query Scope Enforcement
requirements.statement: Every data-access operation involving workspace-scoped data shall enforce workspace filtering at the trusted application or data-access boundary.

requirements.title: � Cross-Workspace Leakage Prevention
requirements.statement: The system shall not include data from one workspace in another workspace’s prompt, memory retrieval, search result, export, notification, usage report, cache response, or administrative view unless an explicit authorized cross-workspace function requires it.

requirements.title: � Cache Segregation
requirements.statement: Workspace-scoped cached data shall be keyed and validated using the applicable workspace boundary.

requirements.title: � Vector Index Segregation
requirements.statement: The system shall segregate vector embeddings and vector-search operations by workspace.

requirements.title: � Keyword Index Segregation
requirements.statement: The system shall segregate keyword and full-text search indexes by workspace or apply equivalent mandatory workspace filters during every search.

requirements.title: � Background Job Segregation
requirements.statement: Every Background Processing Worker job shall include the workspace identifier and authorization-relevant processing scope required to prevent cross-workspace data access.

requirements.title: � Event Segregation
requirements.statement: Workspace-scoped events shall include the stable workspace identifier and shall be consumable only by services authorized for that event scope.

requirements.title: � File Storage Segregation
requirements.statement: Uploaded and derived files shall be stored using a workspace-isolated storage path, namespace, access policy, or equivalent segregation control.

requirements.title: � Provider Connection Segregation
requirements.statement: A provider connection created for one workspace shall not be used to execute requests for another workspace unless an explicit organization-level sharing policy authorizes that use.

requirements.title: � Usage Record Segregation
requirements.statement: Token, cost, quota, and billing records shall be attributed to the workspace and user context responsible for the execution.

requirements.title: � Audit Record Segregation
requirements.statement: Workspace-level audit queries shall return only events authorized for the requesting actor’s workspace and administrative scope.

requirements.title: � Export Segregation
requirements.statement: A workspace export shall contain only the selected workspace’s authorized data and shall exclude data belonging to other workspaces, organizations, or users.

requirements.title: � Workspace Deletion Boundary
requirements.statement: Deleting a workspace shall affect only records owned by or explicitly derived from that workspace.

requirements.title: � Workspace Runtime Configuration
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain workspace-level runtime configuration independently from user-interface state.

requirements.title: � Workspace Configuration Versioning
requirements.statement: The system shall identify the effective workspace configuration used for each runtime execution.

requirements.title: � Workspace State Consistency
requirements.statement: The system shall prevent operations that are incompatible with the current workspace state.

requirements.title: � Workspace Access Audit
requirements.statement: The system shall audit privileged workspace operations, including ownership changes, security-setting changes, provider-connection changes, export requests, restrictions, and deletion actions.

requirements.title: Team Account
requirements.statement: A user with a team account shall be able to create a team workspace with role-based access.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred team account requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Team Invitation
requirements.statement: A team workspace owner shall be able to invite members and assign roles.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred team invitation requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Team Billing
requirements.statement: A team account may support a consolidated billing model.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred team billing requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Export and Deletion
requirements.statement: A user shall be able to export workspace data and request account or workspace deletion.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from export and deletion requirement (Document 02 §16, AC-MVP-012).

requirements.title: File Upload
requirements.statement: The user shall be able to upload files up to 25 MiB. File content shall be extracted as knowledge.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file upload requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: File Metadata
requirements.statement: Uploaded files shall show file name, type, upload date, and file size.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file metadata requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: File Search
requirements.statement: File content shall be searchable through the conversation search interface.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file search requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: File Reprocessing
requirements.statement: The user may request file reprocessing after configuration change.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file reprocessing requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: File Deletion Integrity
requirements.statement: File deletion shall deny the deleted version from retrieval and prompt construction.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file deletion integrity requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: File Compliance
requirements.statement: File handling shall comply with data retention and deletion policies.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred file compliance requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Private Chat
requirements.statement: The user shall be able to create a Private Chat conversation that does not read or write memories by default.
notes: Private Chat prohibits memory reading/writing by default; user may opt into memory reading only
Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from private chat requirement (Document 02 §9).

requirements.title: Model Recommendation
requirements.statement: The router may recommend a model based on task, cost, latency, and capability.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred model recommendation requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Model Routing Constraint
requirements.statement: The user may request routing within a provider family or capability tier.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred routing constraint requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Prompt Enhancement
requirements.statement: The user may use prompt enhancement to optimize a prompt for the selected model.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred prompt enhancement requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: Automatic Provider Fallback
requirements.statement: The router may automatically fall back to an alternative provider or model if the primary request fails.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from deferred automatic provider fallback requirement (Document 02 §17).

requirements.title: � Monthly Service Availability
requirements.statement: The production MVP shall achieve at least 99.5% monthly availability for authenticated workspace and conversation access, excluding approved maintenance windows.

requirements.title: � Runtime Submission Availability
requirements.statement: The system shall achieve at least 99.0% monthly availability for accepting valid runtime execution requests, excluding failures caused solely by unavailable external providers.

requirements.title: � Provider Failure Isolation
requirements.statement: Unavailability of one external AI provider shall not make workspace access, conversation access, memory management, export status, or administrative functions unavailable.

requirements.title: � Degraded Mode
requirements.statement: When an optional subsystem is unavailable, the system shall continue eligible operations using an explicitly documented degraded mode rather than failing unrelated capabilities.

requirements.title: � Streaming Service Availability
requirements.statement: The streaming service shall achieve at least 99.0% monthly availability for authorized active executions.

requirements.title: � Administrative Availability
requirements.statement: Security-critical administrative actions, including account restriction and session revocation, shall remain available during partial failure whenever the identity and authorization services are operational.

requirements.title: � Maintenance Declaration
requirements.statement: Planned maintenance that affects user-facing availability shall be declared before execution and shall identify expected start time, end time, affected capabilities, and recovery criteria.

requirements.title: � Health Check Coverage
requirements.statement: Every production service participating in authentication, runtime execution, streaming, storage, retrieval, or background processing shall expose a machine-readable health state.

requirements.title: � Readiness Enforcement
requirements.statement: A service instance shall not receive production traffic until all mandatory startup dependencies and configuration validations succeed.

requirements.title: � Availability Measurement Integrity
requirements.statement: Availability calculations shall distinguish platform failure, external provider failure, planned maintenance, and client-originated failure.

requirements.title: � Canonical Identifier Stability
requirements.statement: Stable identifiers shall remain unchanged across rename, archive, restore, scaling, migration, and retry operations.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Referential Integrity
requirements.statement: The system shall prevent creation of orphaned workspace-scoped records unless an explicit temporary reconciliation state is defined.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Write Atomicity
requirements.statement: Multi-record state transitions shall either complete atomically or enter a recoverable state with sufficient metadata for reconciliation.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Data Corruption Detection
requirements.statement: Files, exports, and backups shall use integrity metadata sufficient to detect accidental corruption.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Retention Policy Definition
requirements.statement: Every persistent data category shall have a documented retention rule identifying minimum retention, maximum retention, deletion trigger, and legal exception behaviour.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Temporary Artifact Expiration
requirements.statement: Temporary upload artifacts, stream buffers, processing files, and export download artifacts shall expire automatically within their documented validity period.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Deleted Data Non-Retrievability
requirements.statement: Deleted or inactive memories, files, vectors, indexes, and conversations shall not appear in normal retrieval within 60 seconds of the committed state change.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Deletion Completion Evidence
requirements.statement: Permanent deletion shall produce a minimal completion record containing no prohibited user content.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Backup Retention Bound
requirements.statement: Backups containing deleted data shall expire according to a documented retention period not exceeding 35 days for the MVP unless an approved legal exception applies.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Data Migration Verification
requirements.statement: Data migrations shall verify record counts, integrity constraints, identifier preservation, and tenant scope before completion is accepted.

source.section: 16. Data Integrity, Retention, and Deletion Requirements

requirements.title: � Requirement Traceability
requirements.statement: Every MVP Critical and MVP Required non-functional requirement shall map to at least one product principle, functional domain, risk, design control, or acceptance test.

requirements.title: � Change Control
requirements.statement: Changes to approved non-functional thresholds, priorities, or scope shall require documented impact analysis and Product Owner approval.

requirements.title: � Exception Approval
requirements.statement: A temporary exception to an MVP Critical requirement shall identify scope, reason, risk owner, mitigation, expiration date, and approval authority.

requirements.title: � Release Evidence
requirements.statement: Public MVP release shall require an evidence package covering performance, availability, security, isolation, recovery, accessibility, and deletion verification.

requirements.title: � Incident Classification
requirements.statement: Operational incidents shall be classified by severity, affected scope, data impact, user impact, and recovery status.

requirements.title: � Post-Incident Review
requirements.statement: Critical incidents shall receive a documented post-incident review within 5 business days after service restoration.

requirements.title: � Data Processing Inventory
requirements.statement: The system owner shall maintain a current inventory of personal data categories, processing purposes, storage locations, external providers, retention rules, and deletion mechanisms.

requirements.title: � Baseline Approval
requirements.statement: This specification shall not become the implementation baseline until approved by the Product Owner.

requirements.title: � Workspace Scope Enforcement
requirements.statement: Every protected data operation shall enforce workspace scope at a trusted server or data-access boundary.

requirements.title: � Cross-Workspace Leakage Prohibition
requirements.statement: The tolerated cross-workspace data leakage rate shall be zero.

requirements.title: � Identifier Non-Authority
requirements.statement: Possession of a resource identifier shall not grant access without an independently validated authorized workspace context.

requirements.title: � Cache Isolation
requirements.statement: Shared caches shall include immutable tenant scope in cache keys and shall not return protected data without authorization revalidation where required.

requirements.title: � Search Isolation
requirements.statement: Vector, keyword, hybrid, and administrative searches shall enforce tenant scope before results are returned or injected into runtime context.

requirements.title: � Queue Isolation
requirements.statement: Every workspace-scoped background job shall retain an immutable workspace identifier from creation through retry, reassignment, and completion.

requirements.title: � File Isolation
requirements.statement: File object references and temporary download links shall not authorize access outside the selected workspace.

requirements.title: � Provider Credential Isolation
requirements.statement: A provider credential associated with one workspace shall never authenticate a request for another workspace unless an explicit organization-level sharing policy authorizes it.

requirements.title: � Export Isolation
requirements.statement: Export integrity validation shall fail the export when any included record cannot be proven to belong to the authorized export scope.

requirements.title: � Isolation Regression Gate
requirements.statement: No release shall be approved when any automated tenant-isolation regression test fails.

requirements.title: � Modular Service Boundaries
requirements.statement: Identity, workspace, runtime, memory, provider routing, streaming, search, usage, notification, and background-processing responsibilities shall have explicit module or service boundaries.

requirements.title: � Configuration Externalization
requirements.statement: Environment-specific endpoints, credentials, timeouts, limits, pricing data, and feature controls shall not require source-code modification.

requirements.title: � Schema Migration Safety
requirements.statement: Production data migrations shall be versioned, reversible where practical, and validated against rollback or forward-recovery procedures.

requirements.title: � Backward Compatibility
requirements.statement: An API change that breaks an approved client contract shall require a new version or an explicitly approved migration window.

requirements.title: � Automated Test Coverage
requirements.statement: MVP Critical requirements shall have automated verification where technically feasible, and any manual-only verification shall have documented justification.

requirements.title: � Code Quality Gate
requirements.statement: Release candidates shall pass approved static analysis, formatting, type checking where supported, dependency scanning, and automated test suites.

requirements.title: � Operational Runbooks
requirements.statement: Authentication failure, provider outage, queue backlog, data restoration, tenant-isolation incident, export failure, and deletion failure shall each have an approved operational runbook before public MVP release.

requirements.title: � Support Diagnostics
requirements.statement: Authorized support personnel shall be able to inspect non-secret operational status using correlation identifiers without requiring direct database access.

requirements.title: � Documentation Currency
requirements.statement: A release shall not be approved when implemented behaviour materially differs from the approved PRD, FRS, NFRS, API, or security documentation.

requirements.title: � Change Traceability
requirements.statement: Every implementation task, code change, and test case affecting MVP behaviour shall reference at least one approved requirement identifier.

requirements.title: � Correlation Identifier
requirements.statement: Every authenticated request, runtime execution, provider request, stream, background job, export, and deletion workflow shall have a traceable correlation identifier.

requirements.title: � Structured Logging
requirements.statement: Production services shall emit structured machine-readable logs containing service, environment, severity, event category, timestamp, and correlation context.

requirements.title: � Secret-Free Telemetry
requirements.statement: Logs, metrics, traces, and alerts shall exclude reusable secrets and unnecessary complete user content.

requirements.title: � Runtime Stage Metrics
requirements.statement: The system shall measure request count, success rate, error rate, and latency for each material runtime stage.

requirements.title: � Provider Metrics
requirements.statement: The system shall measure provider availability, timeout rate, rate-limit rate, fallback rate, and usage volume by provider and model.

requirements.title: � Queue Metrics
requirements.statement: Background-processing observability shall include queue depth, oldest-job age, processing rate, retry count, dead-letter count, and failure rate.

requirements.title: � Data-Layer Metrics
requirements.statement: The system shall monitor database error rate, connection saturation, query latency, storage utilization, index health, and replication or backup health.

requirements.title: � Alerting Time
requirements.statement: Critical security, availability, data-loss, cross-workspace isolation, and deletion-integrity events shall generate an operational alert within 5 minutes of detection.

requirements.title: � Audit Event Durability
requirements.statement: Security-significant and destructive audit events shall be durably recorded before the corresponding operation is reported complete where technically feasible.

requirements.title: � Audit Immutability
requirements.statement: Ordinary application and administrative workflows shall not modify existing audit-event content.

requirements.title: � Audit Search Performance
requirements.statement: Authorized audit searches over 90 days of MVP-scale data shall complete within 5 seconds at the 95th percentile.

requirements.title: � Monitoring Retention
requirements.statement: Operational metrics shall be retained for at least 90 days and security-relevant audit events for at least 365 days unless a stricter approved policy applies.

requirements.title: � API Acceptance Latency
requirements.statement: For authenticated non-streaming API operations that do not invoke an external AI provider or background job, the system shall complete 95% of valid requests within 500 milliseconds and 99% within 1,500 milliseconds under the approved MVP reference load.

requirements.title: � Message Acceptance Latency
requirements.statement: The system shall acknowledge 95% of valid user-message submissions within 750 milliseconds before external provider response generation begins.

requirements.title: � Runtime Preparation Latency
requirements.statement: Excluding external provider latency, the runtime pipeline from accepted message to provider-request dispatch shall complete within 1,500 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for direct-route executions and within 3,000 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for enhanced-route executions under the MVP reference load.

requirements.title: � Streaming First-Event Latency
requirements.statement: After the first valid provider response event is received, the system shall emit the corresponding client stream event within 250 milliseconds at the 95th percentile.

requirements.title: � Stream Relay Overhead
requirements.statement: The platform-added median latency between receipt of a provider content delta and emission of that delta to an authorized client shall not exceed 100 milliseconds under the MVP reference load.

requirements.title: � Memory Retrieval Latency
requirements.statement: Memory retrieval for an eligible runtime execution shall complete within 750 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for workspaces containing up to 100,000 active memory records.

requirements.title: � Search Latency
requirements.statement: Workspace-scoped keyword or vector searches returning no more than 50 results shall complete within 1,000 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for approved MVP dataset sizes.

requirements.title: � Conversation Listing Latency
requirements.statement: Paginated conversation-list requests with page sizes up to 100 records shall complete within 500 milliseconds at the 95th percentile under the approved MVP reference load.

requirements.title: � Memory Listing Latency
requirements.statement: Paginated memory-list requests with page sizes up to 100 records shall complete within 750 milliseconds at the 95th percentile under the approved MVP reference load.

requirements.title: � Administrative Query Latency
requirements.statement: Authorized usage, audit, and administrative list queries over a 30-day period shall complete within 2,000 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for approved MVP data volumes.

requirements.title: � File Upload Acceptance
requirements.statement: The system shall acknowledge successful receipt of a supported file within 2,000 milliseconds after the final upload byte is received, excluding file-parsing time.

requirements.title: � TXT Processing Time
requirements.statement: Plain-text files up to 10 MB shall reach a terminal parsed or failed state within 60 seconds for 95% of valid files under normal MVP load.

requirements.title: � PDF Processing Time
requirements.statement: Machine-readable PDF files up to 100 pages or 25 MB, whichever limit is reached first, shall reach a terminal parsed or failed state within 180 seconds for 95% of valid files under normal MVP load.

requirements.title: � Export Generation Time
requirements.statement: Workspace exports containing up to 5 GB of eligible data shall reach a ready or failed state within 30 minutes for 95% of requests under normal MVP load.

requirements.title: � Cancellation Responsiveness
requirements.statement: After an authorized cancellation request is accepted, the system shall stop presenting additional normal generation output within 500 milliseconds at the 95th percentile.

requirements.title: � Snapshot-Lock Non-Blocking Behaviour
requirements.statement: Active background processing for a preceding message shall add no more than 100 milliseconds of platform latency to acceptance of a subsequent message that uses snapshot-lock behaviour.

requirements.title: � Background Queue Dispatch
requirements.statement: Eligible background work shall be enqueued within 2 seconds after synchronous runtime finalization for 99% of completed executions.

requirements.title: � Token Counting Overhead
requirements.statement: Local token estimation and budget calculation shall add no more than 250 milliseconds at the 95th percentile for prompts up to the approved maximum MVP context size.

requirements.title: � Performance Degradation Bound
requirements.statement: At 80% of approved MVP capacity, 95th-percentile latency for critical runtime operations shall not exceed twice the corresponding baseline threshold defined in this section.

requirements.title: � Performance Test Reproducibility
requirements.statement: Every release candidate shall include a reproducible performance-test report identifying dataset size, concurrency, payload profile, environment, and percentile results.

requirements.title: � Provider-Neutral Domain Model
requirements.statement: Canonical conversations, messages, memories, usage records, and runtime executions shall not require one provider’s proprietary identifier as their primary identity.

requirements.title: � Provider Adapter Boundary
requirements.statement: Provider-specific authentication, request formatting, streaming events, errors, usage reporting, and model metadata shall be isolated behind an explicit provider integration boundary.

requirements.title: � Provider Replacement
requirements.statement: Adding or replacing a supported provider shall not require modification of canonical workspace, conversation, message, or memory records.

requirements.title: � Error Normalization
requirements.statement: Provider-specific failures shall be normalized into stable internal categories before they are consumed by user-facing or orchestration logic.

requirements.title: � Pricing Version Independence
requirements.statement: Provider pricing updates shall be deployable as versioned configuration without rewriting historical usage records.

requirements.title: � Model Catalogue Refresh
requirements.statement: Provider model catalogues shall be refreshable without application redeployment where the selected architecture supports dynamic metadata refresh.

requirements.title: � Data Export Portability
requirements.statement: User exports shall use documented, machine-readable formats for structured data and shall preserve stable identifiers and provenance where permitted.

requirements.title: � Infrastructure Portability
requirements.statement: Core business rules shall not depend directly on one cloud vendor’s proprietary runtime semantics without an explicit abstraction or approved exception.

requirements.title: � Data Minimization
requirements.statement: The system shall collect, retain, and process only data required for documented product, security, billing, legal, or operational purposes.

requirements.title: � Purpose Limitation
requirements.statement: User content shall not be used for an unrelated purpose without explicit policy authority and applicable user consent.

requirements.title: � Provider Data Disclosure Transparency
requirements.statement: Before transmitting user content to an external AI provider, the system shall make the applicable provider identity and execution context discoverable to the user.

requirements.title: � Export Completeness
requirements.statement: A user export shall include all eligible user-controlled data within the requested scope and shall exclude secrets and inaccessible data.

requirements.title: � Deletion Effectiveness
requirements.statement: Permanently deleted content shall become inaccessible through normal APIs, search, retrieval, runtime context, exports, and administrative views immediately after deletion completion.

requirements.title: � Derived Data Deletion
requirements.statement: Deletion workflows shall remove or invalidate derived embeddings, indexes, caches, summaries, and temporary artifacts associated exclusively with deleted source data.

requirements.title: � Notification Privacy
requirements.statement: External notifications shall not include message content, memory content, provider credentials, export contents, or other sensitive workspace data.

requirements.title: � Support Access Limitation
requirements.statement: Support access to private user content shall require explicit operational purpose, minimum necessary scope, time limitation, and audit recording.

requirements.title: � Data Location Recording
requirements.statement: The system shall maintain an inventory of production systems and external providers that store or process protected user data.

requirements.title: � Privacy Incident Traceability
requirements.statement: A privacy-relevant incident shall be traceable to affected workspaces, data categories, processing systems, and time ranges without exposing unrelated user data.

requirements.title: � Recovery Point Objective
requirements.statement: The production MVP shall maintain a recovery point objective of no more than 15 minutes for canonical workspace, conversation, message, memory, provider-connection metadata, usage, and configuration data.

requirements.title: � Recovery Time Objective
requirements.statement: The production MVP shall maintain a recovery time objective of no more than 4 hours for restoration of core authenticated workspace and conversation capabilities after a recoverable platform-wide failure.

requirements.title: � Backup Frequency
requirements.statement: Canonical production data shall be backed up or continuously replicated at a frequency sufficient to satisfy NFR-REC-001.

requirements.title: � Backup Encryption
requirements.statement: Backups containing protected data shall be encrypted in transit and at rest and shall use access controls separate from ordinary application access.

requirements.title: � Backup Restoration Test
requirements.statement: A representative production backup restoration shall be tested at least once every 90 days.

requirements.title: � Deletion Reapplication After Restore
requirements.statement: Restored environments shall reapply valid deletion records before normal user access is enabled.

requirements.title: � Retry Bound
requirements.statement: Automated retries shall use bounded attempt counts, backoff, and terminal failure handling and shall not retry indefinitely.

requirements.title: � Circuit Breaking
requirements.statement: Repeated dependency failure shall trigger a controlled circuit-break or equivalent protection before cascading failure affects unrelated operations.

requirements.title: � Stale Job Recovery
requirements.statement: Background jobs with no progress for longer than their configured execution timeout shall be detected and moved to retry, failure, or reconciliation within 5 minutes.

requirements.title: � Partial Stream Recovery
requirements.statement: After service interruption, persisted partial response content shall remain distinguishable from completed content and shall not be duplicated during reconnection.

requirements.title: � Region Failure Readiness
requirements.statement: The architecture shall not permanently bind user data or stable identifiers to one compute instance or one non-recoverable runtime node.

requirements.title: � Recovery Audit
requirements.statement: Backup restoration, replay, failover, deletion reapplication, and reconciliation actions shall generate audit evidence.

requirements.title: � Durable Message Acceptance
requirements.statement: No message shall be reported as accepted unless its canonical content and required identifiers are durably stored or placed in a recoverable write-ahead state.

requirements.title: � Duplicate Execution Prevention
requirements.statement: Under client retry, network retry, and service retry conditions, the system shall not create duplicate provider executions for the same valid idempotency scope.

requirements.title: � Terminal-State Consistency
requirements.statement: Completed, failed, and cancelled execution states shall remain mutually exclusive and shall not transition to another terminal state after finalization.

requirements.title: � Usage Record Consistency
requirements.statement: Every accepted provider execution shall have exactly one authoritative usage record or one explicit reconciliation record indicating why final usage is unavailable.

requirements.title: � Cost Calculation Reproducibility
requirements.statement: Recalculation using the recorded pricing version and usage values shall reproduce the stored provider-cost result within the approved numeric precision.

requirements.title: � Ordering Consistency
requirements.statement: Message, stream-event, audit-event, and job ordering shall remain deterministic within their declared scope.

requirements.title: � Memory Provenance Integrity
requirements.statement: Every active extracted memory shall retain at least one valid provenance reference or an explicit record that the memory was independently confirmed by an authorized user.

requirements.title: � Index Consistency
requirements.statement: Canonical records and retrieval indexes shall not remain materially inconsistent for longer than 60 seconds after a successful committed update under normal operating conditions.

requirements.title: � Partial Failure Visibility
requirements.statement: A multi-step operation that cannot complete fully shall expose a controlled intermediate, pending, failed, or reconciliation state and shall not report false success.

requirements.title: � Background Job Idempotency
requirements.statement: Retrying the same background job identity shall not create duplicate active memories, indexes, notifications, exports, usage records, or deletion actions.

requirements.title: � Clock Consistency
requirements.statement: Security, retention, expiration, billing, and ordering decisions shall use server-controlled time synchronized within 2 seconds across production services.

requirements.title: � Release Regression Protection
requirements.statement: A release candidate shall not be approved when automated regression tests fail for any MVP Critical functional or non-functional requirement.

requirements.title: � Concurrent User Capacity
requirements.statement: The MVP architecture shall support at least 1,000 concurrently authenticated users without violating the critical latency thresholds defined in Section 4.

requirements.title: � Concurrent Runtime Capacity
requirements.statement: The system shall support at least 200 concurrently active provider executions across workspaces without cross-execution data leakage or uncontrolled queue growth.

requirements.title: � Workspace Data Capacity
requirements.statement: A single workspace shall support at least 1,000,000 messages, 100,000 active memories, 10,000 files, and 10,000 runtime executions per month without requiring a data-model redesign.

requirements.title: � Horizontal Runtime Scaling
requirements.statement: Runtime, streaming, search, and background-processing services shall support horizontal scaling without requiring workspace-specific code changes.

requirements.title: � Stateless Request Handling
requirements.statement: User-facing request services should avoid mandatory instance-local session state unless equivalent shared or recoverable state is available.

requirements.title: � Queue Backpressure
requirements.statement: Background queues shall apply configurable backpressure before queue depth causes unbounded memory growth, data loss, or synchronous runtime failure.

requirements.title: � Per-Workspace Fairness
requirements.statement: One workspace shall not consume more than its configured share of shared runtime, search, storage, or background-processing capacity when doing so would starve other workspaces.

requirements.title: � Storage Growth Monitoring
requirements.statement: The system shall measure storage growth by workspace and data category and shall generate an operational warning before any managed storage reaches 80% of its approved capacity.

requirements.title: � Capacity Forecast
requirements.statement: Production operations shall maintain a rolling 90-day capacity forecast for request volume, storage, vector index size, and background-job volume.

requirements.title: � Scaling Without Identifier Change
requirements.statement: Scaling, partitioning, reindexing, or migration shall not change stable user-facing resource identifiers.

requirements.title: � Encrypted Transport
requirements.statement: All production client, service, database, queue, object-storage, and provider communications carrying protected data shall use approved encrypted transport.

requirements.title: � Encryption at Rest
requirements.statement: Provider credentials, authentication secrets, user content, memories, files, exports, and backups shall be encrypted at rest using approved cryptographic controls.

requirements.title: � Secret Separation
requirements.statement: Encryption keys and reusable secrets shall be stored separately from the protected application records they secure.

requirements.title: � Least Privilege
requirements.statement: Human and service identities shall receive only the minimum permissions required for their documented responsibilities.

requirements.title: � Default Deny
requirements.statement: Access to protected resources and actions shall be denied when an explicit permission cannot be established.

requirements.title: � Administrative Strong Authentication
requirements.statement: Production administrative access shall require multi-factor authentication or an equivalent elevated assurance mechanism.

requirements.title: � Sensitive Action Re-authentication
requirements.statement: Account deletion, workspace deletion, provider-credential replacement, complete export, and session-wide revocation shall require recent authentication not older than 15 minutes or an approved additional verification step.

requirements.title: � Session Protection
requirements.statement: Browser session cookies shall use Secure, HttpOnly, and an approved SameSite policy, and reusable session credentials shall never appear in user-visible URLs.

requirements.title: � Session Expiration
requirements.statement: The default authenticated session shall expire after no more than 30 days absolute lifetime and no more than 24 hours of inactivity unless a stricter policy applies.

requirements.title: � Password Protection
requirements.statement: Password-based authentication shall use an approved adaptive one-way hashing function with unique salts and configurable work factors.

requirements.title: � Password Minimum Length
requirements.statement: Where password authentication is enabled, the minimum password length shall be at least 12 characters.

requirements.title: � Authentication Abuse Protection
requirements.statement: Authentication, recovery, verification, provider validation, export, and deletion endpoints shall enforce configurable rate limits and abuse controls.

requirements.title: � Credential Logging Prohibition
requirements.statement: Plaintext passwords, session secrets, recovery tokens, provider API keys, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, and complete private credentials shall never be written to application logs, traces, analytics, or user-visible errors.

requirements.title: � Input Validation
requirements.statement: All externally supplied data shall be validated for type, size, format, ownership, scope, and supported values before durable mutation or provider execution.

requirements.title: � Untrusted Content Isolation
requirements.statement: Retrieved memories, files, provider responses, and search results shall be treated as untrusted content and shall not override higher-authority instructions.

requirements.title: � File Processing Isolation
requirements.statement: File parsing shall execute with no direct access to provider credentials, unrelated workspace data, or unrestricted host resources.

requirements.title: � Security Event Detection
requirements.statement: Repeated authentication failures, unauthorized access attempts, cross-workspace scope failures, credential misuse, and abnormal administrative actions shall generate security events within 60 seconds.

requirements.title: � Vulnerability Remediation
requirements.statement: Confirmed critical vulnerabilities affecting production shall be mitigated within 24 hours and high-severity vulnerabilities within 7 calendar days, unless an approved risk exception exists.

requirements.title: � Dependency Security Review
requirements.statement: Production dependencies shall be scanned for known vulnerabilities before release and at least once every 24 hours thereafter.

requirements.title: � Security Testing Gate
requirements.statement: Public MVP release shall require passing authentication, authorization, tenant-isolation, secret-exposure, file-processing, injection-resistance, and destructive-operation security tests.

requirements.title: � Accessibility Standard
requirements.statement: User-facing MVP web interfaces shall conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA for supported workflows, excluding documented third-party limitations outside platform control.

requirements.title: � Keyboard Operation
requirements.statement: Core authentication, conversation, message, memory, provider, export, and deletion workflows shall be operable using a keyboard alone.

requirements.title: � Error Comprehension
requirements.statement: User-facing errors shall state the failed action, a stable error category, and an actionable next step where one exists, without exposing sensitive internals.

requirements.title: � Destructive Action Confirmation
requirements.statement: Permanent deletion and credential-removal actions shall require explicit confirmation that clearly identifies the affected scope and reversibility.

requirements.title: � Streaming State Visibility
requirements.statement: The interface shall distinguish sending, streaming, complete, failed, incomplete, and cancelled message states.

requirements.title: � Provider and Model Visibility
requirements.statement: The effective provider and model used for a completed execution shall be discoverable by the user.

requirements.title: � Long-Running Operation Visibility
requirements.statement: File processing, export generation, deletion, and background-review workflows shall expose a current state and terminal success or failure state.

requirements.title: � Timezone Display
requirements.statement: User-facing timestamps shall be displayed in the configured user timezone while preserving canonical server time internally.

requirements.title: Service Ownership and Incident Response
requirements.statement: Every service, job class, data store, integration, dashboard, alert, and runbook shall have a named technical owner with escalation path. Provider failures shall be measured separately from Gexor-controlled availability. A documented incident response process with severity classification, communication template, and post-incident review shall exist before public beta.
acceptanceCriteria: Service catalog exists with named owners

requirements.title: Process Recovery
requirements.statement: A service process failure shall not cause permanent data loss. In-flight executions shall be recoverable or gracefully failed.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from process recovery resilience requirement (Document 14).

requirements.title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
requirements.statement: Transient failures shall be retried with exponential backoff and jitter.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from retry with exponential backoff requirement (Document 14).

requirements.title: Background Job Recovery
requirements.statement: Failed background jobs shall be retried according to policy. Jobs exceeding the retry limit shall be recorded and escalated.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from background job recovery resilience requirement (Document 14).

requirements.title: Release Exit Criteria for MVPs
requirements.statement: Before any MVP release: All MVP critical/required requirements must have passing evidence. No open Severity 1 or unaccepted Severity 2 defects. Security, tenant-isolation, accessibility, performance, resilience, migration, rollback, restore gates must pass. Alert/runbook checks and operational readiness must pass. Product, Engineering, QA, Security, Operations must approve.
acceptanceCriteria: All defined gates pass before release
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: WCAG 2.2 AA Accessibility for MVP Surface
requirements.statement: The MVP user-facing surface shall meet WCAG 2.2 AA requirements including: keyboard operable, visible focus indicators, logical focus order, skip links, semantic headings/landmarks, status changes using live regions (not every streamed token), color not sole signal, text contrast AA, 400% zoom/reflow, and reduced-motion support.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing

requirements.title: Keyboard Navigation and Screen Reader
requirements.statement: The primary user interface shall be keyboard-operable and compatible with screen-reader software.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from keyboard and screen-reader accessibility requirement (Document 11 §8).

requirements.title: Visual Contrast and Scalability
requirements.statement: Text and interactive elements shall meet WCAG AA contrast ratios. The interface shall support browser-level text scaling up to 200% without loss of functionality.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from visual contrast and scalability requirement (Document 11 §8).

requirements.title: Memory Inspectability
requirements.statement: Memory controls and runtime details shall be accessible through standard input methods.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from memory inspectability requirement (Document 11 §8).

requirements.title: Streaming Announcements
requirements.statement: Streaming response updates shall use live regions for screen-reader announcements but shall not announce every individual token.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from streaming announcements accessibility requirement (Document 11 §8).

requirements.title: Non-Color Indicators
requirements.statement: Status and state information shall not rely solely on color. Text labels, icons, or patterns shall be used.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from non-color indicators requirement (Document 11 §8).

requirements.title: Reduced Motion
requirements.statement: Animated transitions shall respect the user's reduced-motion preference.
notes: Synthetic ID generated for memory indexing. Derived from reduced-motion preference requirement (Document 11 §8).
